summary: Productized M7 map presentation on existing read models without changing sim/protocol/save authority. The map renderer now derives hover, selected, reachable, blocked/overloaded, label LOD, pan, and rebuild metadata as disposable render-plan state; Web/UI localize map labels, legend, hover details, pan controls, and list-map synchronization through the accepted i18n foundation. Storybook and focused tests cover normal/empty/error/extreme/narrow/bilingual map presentation, GPU/context rebuild expectations, player-mode debug hiding, no console errors, and viewport/keyboard flow.

tests_run.result: Initial status was clean. Final status listed only allowed-path modifications in .storybook, apps/web, packages/map-renderer, packages/ui, tests, the active task JSON, and this handoff.
Task registry listed M7-MAP-PRESENTATION-QUALITY-001 as REVIEW/client_engineer.
Current task no longer appears as READY after REVIEW status; next listed candidates were M7-PLAYER-GUIDANCE-LITE-001 and M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-MAP-PRESENTATION-QUALITY-001
tests_run.result: JSON parse/shape OK: id M7-MAP-PRESENTATION-QUALITY-001, status REVIEW, route_to systems_architect,test_engineer,qa_reviewer, human_gate_required false.

Vitest map-renderer suite passed: 1 file, 5 tests. Coverage includes render-plan hover/selected/reachable/blocked/overloaded/pan states, label LOD, rebuild from replace-read-model delta, and no sim-core/WorldState import.
Vitest UI suite passed: 1 file, 16 tests. Coverage includes map legend, pan controls, hover details, player-mode debug hiding, localized English/zh-CN shell and no raw route/reason/debug labels.
Vitest web suite passed: 1 file, 9 tests. Coverage includes localized map presentation labels, player shell debug separation, and rebuildable Pixi scene shell.
Vite web build passed. Existing large chunk warning remained.
Storybook static build passed and cleaned .tmp/storybook-static. Existing large chunk/plugin timing warnings remained.

Playwright Chromium suite passed: 19 tests. The map flow covers mode switching, zoom, pan, hover tooltip/list sync, selected district/settlement sync, no debug labels in player mode, no console/page errors, keyboard focus including pan controls, and required desktop/narrow viewport overflow checks.
Format check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.

decisions: Kept Pixi and canvas as rebuildable render caches. Map hover, pan, selected/reachable/blocked/overloaded presentation fields are derived from ClientMapReadModelSnapshot plus local viewport UI state and are not authoritative domain state.

Localized map label presentation by creating a Web-shell presentation snapshot with localized district/settlement displayName fields before mounting Pixi; ids, geometry, routes, read-model revision, and simulation snapshot are not mutated.
Added DOM legend and hover detail text through i18n keys instead of duplicating domain/pathfinding rules. Route summaries reuse existing route read-model fields and formatting helpers.

Used existing M7 design token colors and neutral placeholder CSS/Pixi tokens for map strokes, route states, settlement markers, tooltip frame, and legend swatches; no culture-specific final art or geography facts were added.
Added pan controls as local viewport offsets with bounded clamping. Pan affects only renderer placement metadata and does not change selection, route authority, command state, or save state.

Routed REVIEW to systems_architect,test_engineer,qa_reviewer because this task has map authority boundary risk and explicit completion routing to test/QA. No Electron files or preload/IPC surfaces changed, so security_reviewer routing is not requested.
risks: The Web build and Storybook build still emit existing large chunk warnings; builds pass and this task did not add a new production dependency.
Map labels are localized through presentation displayName copies. This is intentionally not a content-name localization system and should be reviewed as UI presentation only.
Pixi hover is covered through renderer callback wiring and e2e list-hover synchronization; deeper visual pixel inspection of hover strokes remains a possible test_engineer follow-up if desired.
No Electron renderer/main/preload files changed, so Electron security posture was not re-reviewed here.
requested_action: Please review M7-MAP-PRESENTATION-QUALITY-001 for map authority boundaries, test coverage, localization/player-mode presentation, and QA acceptance. Do not mark CLOSED until reviewer acceptance is complete.
